prompt: Add recurring task support to the task scheduler

turns.content:
<task>
Add recurring task support — tasks can be scheduled to repeat every N seconds. The scheduler should re-enqueue them after execution.
</task>

1	import time
   2	from typing import Callable, List, Optional
   3	from dataclasses import dataclass
   4	
   5	@dataclass
   6	class Task:
   7	    name: str
   8	    fn: Callable
   9	    run_at: float
  10	
  11	class Scheduler:
  12	    def __init__(self):
  13	        self.tasks: List[Task] = []
  14	        self.executed: List[str] = []
  15	
  16	    def schedule(self, name: str, fn: Callable, delay: float = 0):
  17	        self.tasks.append(Task(name=name, fn=fn, run_at=time.time() + delay))
  18	
  19	    def tick(self):
  20	        now = time.time()
  21	        ready = [t for t in self.tasks if t.run_at <= now]
  22	        for task in ready:
  23	            task.fn()
  24	            self.executed.append(task.name)
  25	            self.tasks.remove(task)

arguments.summary: Read scheduler/core/scheduler.py — Task(name, fn, run_at), Scheduler with schedule(name, fn, delay) and tick(). Tasks are one-shot (removed after execution). Need to add interval field and re-enqueue recurring tasks.

next_steps.description: Add interval field to Task and re-enqueue logic in tick() for recurring tasks

arguments.summary: Added recurring task support to scheduler/core/scheduler.py. Task has optional interval field. schedule() accepts interval param. tick() re-enqueues recurring tasks with new run_at = now + interval. One-shot tasks still removed. All 4 tests pass.
arguments.final_answer: Added recurring task support to scheduler. Tasks with an interval are re-enqueued after execution. One-shot tasks work unchanged. All 4 tests pass.
